data.title: S&P wobbles near record high as investors look for firm direction in 'overvalued' stock market
data.description:
Major U.S. equity market averages wavered Tuesday as investors kicked off the holiday-shortened trading week with attention on geopolitics, and sought firm direction with stocks near or at record highs.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, circling near its record high of 6,128.18 notched in late January. The Dow (DJI) was -0.2%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also near even, while the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) reached a record high of 22,191.37 before slipping.

The tech-concentrated Nasdaq-100 (NDX) has gained more than 5% this year. “[An] impressive feat considering the volatility from DeepSeek, tariffs, and interest rates,” Adam Turnquist, chief technical strategist at LPL Financial, said in a Tuesday note. The S&P 500 (SP500) has advanced 4% so far this year.

Tuesday's gainers included Intel (INTC), up +9 after a Wall Street Journal report that Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and Broadcom (AVGO) are separately looking at acquiring parts of the chipmaker.

As equities march higher, nine in 10 global money managers consider U.S. stocks as overvalued, according to Bank of America's latest survey. The survey of 205 panelists with $482B in assets under management, showed that 89% of respondents see U.S. stocks (SPY)(QQQ)(DIA) as overpriced, the most since at least April 2001.

In the bond market, global rates rose. European bonds were the driver, with prices falling on speculation that the market would see new issuance as countries spent more on defense.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.53% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.29%.

U.S. and Russia talks over ending Russia's war against Ukraine began Tuesday in Saudi Arabia. Ukraine and European Union nations were not present.

Deutsche Bank strategist Jim Reid said "potential implications are huge after the events of the last five days where first the US froze out Europe in announcing the start of talks with Russia, and second with the broadside against Europe from US Vice President JD Vance."

"That all this is occurring within a week of the German general election (this Sunday) means that Europe might start to move down a very different path within weeks to the pre-existing one," he said.

On the macro front, the February Empire State Manufacturing Index surprised to the upside.

"The initial reaction (Monday) was to price in more European defense spending with bonds selling off and equities rising, especially in the defense sector," Reid said.

data.title: Medtronic targets $1B cardiovascular growth in FY25 with PFA expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Geoff Martha highlighted mid-single-digit revenue growth for the ninth consecutive quarter, driven by strong performances in cardiac ablation solutions, leadless pacing, neuromodulation, and diabetes. He emphasized the significant potential of renal denervation and stated, "We are stacking growth drivers on top of growth drivers with groundbreaking innovation in some of the most attractive markets in MedTech."

  • The cardiovascular portfolio grew mid-single-digits, with cardiac ablation solutions reporting 22% growth, driven by the Pulse Field Ablation (PFA) products, Affera and PulseSelect. Management anticipates this portfolio to reach $1 billion in sales for FY25 and sees a path to $2 billion as PFA adoption expands globally.

  • Structural heart products, including the Evolut FX+ TAVR system, showed high-single-digit growth excluding congenital issues. Upcoming data presentations, such as the five-year low-risk data and two-year SMART trial results, are expected to further strengthen this segment.

  • The neuroscience portfolio grew mid-single-digits, with cranial and spinal technologies achieving 5% growth globally and 8% in the U.S. Neuromodulation grew 13%, supported by closed-loop sensing technology in pain stim and brain modulation devices.

  • CFO Gary Corona reported Q3 revenue of $8.3 billion with adjusted EPS of $1.39. He noted, "The EPS beat was driven by better-than-expected operating profit on stronger gross margins and a better-than-expected tax rate."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated FY25 organic revenue growth guidance of 4.75% to 5% and non-GAAP diluted EPS guidance of $5.44 to $5.50. Q4 revenue and EPS growth are expected to accelerate, with cardiovascular portfolio growth driving the momentum.

  • The company anticipates high-single-digit EPS growth in the back half of FY25, with strong operating margin expansion.

  • Geoff Martha expressed confidence in the renal denervation opportunity, citing Medicare coverage expected within eight months and a large addressable U.S. patient population.

Financial Results

  • Q3 revenue reached $8.3 billion, representing 4.1% organic growth. Adjusted EPS was $1.39 compared to analysts' estimate of $1.36.

  • Gross margin improved to 66.6%, aided by cost efficiency programs, disciplined pricing, and favorable business mix.

  • Neuroscience and cardiovascular portfolios contributed mid-single-digit growth, offsetting slower performance in medical surgical due to U.S. distributor dynamics.

  • Operating margin rose to 26.2%, up 100 basis points year-over-year, reflecting operational improvements and cost controls.

Q&A

  • Patrick Wood, Morgan Stanley: Questioned FY26 growth trajectory. Geoff Martha affirmed commitment to profitable growth, reiterating FY25 guidance of mid-single-digit revenue and high-single-digit EPS growth.

  • Larry Biegelsen, Wells Fargo: Asked about cardiac ablation solutions growth drivers and Affera ramp-up. Sean Salmon stated, "Affera’s integrated mapping and ablation capabilities are driving high demand... The new factory in Galway has significantly improved supply."

  • Travis Steed, Bank of America: Inquired about balancing growth investments and margin expansion. Geoff Martha emphasized gross margin improvements and cost controls as key enablers for sustained investment in growth drivers.

  • Vijay Kumar, Evercore ISI: Asked about Affera revenue potential. Sean Salmon noted, "Revenue per case is higher due to additional components like mapping catheters, offering hospitals cost savings with integrated solutions."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism regarding U.S. surgical performance and distributor inventory dynamics but were optimistic about cardiovascular and neuromodulation growth drivers.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in prepared remarks, emphasizing operational improvements, supply chain stability, and product innovation. Geoff Martha frequently highlighted "stacking growth drivers" and "disciplined pricing." However, some defensive language appeared in responses to surgical business inquiries.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management showed increased confidence in cardiovascular growth and PFA adoption.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 saw continued mid-single-digit revenue growth, consistent with Q2. However, Q3 highlighted stronger cardiovascular performance, with PFA sales accelerating.

  • Q3 adjusted EPS growth of 6.9% marked an improvement over Q2’s 8% constant currency EPS growth, reflecting operational leverage and margin expansion.

  • Management’s tone was more optimistic regarding cardiovascular and diabetes segments, while surgical performance challenges persisted.

Risks and Concerns

  • U.S. surgical portfolio faced headwinds from distributor inventory adjustments, impacting performance. Management expects this to normalize in Q1 FY26.

  • Peripheral vascular business declined due to China’s volume-based procurement reforms, introducing volatility.

  • Analysts raised concerns about new growth drivers' potential impact on margins, given early-stage investments in Hugo robotics and Affera ablation systems.

Final Takeaway

Medtronic reported strong Q3 results, driven by cardiovascular and neuromodulation growth. Management emphasized the significant potential of PFA and renal denervation, targeting $1 billion in FY25 cardiovascular sales. With high-single-digit EPS growth expected in the second half, the company remains focused on operational leverage, disciplined pricing, and innovation to sustain long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Meta announces new developer conference LlamaCon due to 'unprecedented growth'
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) announced a new developer conference dubbed LlamaCon, which is scheduled for April 29.

Meta's Llama model was downloaded more than 650M times in 2024, and Meta AI reached about 600M average daily users by the end of the year, the social media company reported. The surge in popularity prompted the Mark Zuckerberg-led company to create LlamaCon.

"Following the unprecedented growth and momentum of our open-source Llama collection of models and tools, we're excited to introduce LlamaCon—a developer conference for 2025 that will take place April 29," Meta said.

Meta plans to reveal its latest open source, artificial intelligence-powered tools to help developers build apps and other products at LlamaCon.

The company also announced the date of this year's Meta Connect, which is slated for September 17-18.

"As in years past, we'll share the latest and greatest in Meta Horizon updates, peel back the curtain on tomorrow's tech, and give XR devs the tools they need to help build for the next computing platform today," the company said.

Last year, Meta revealed its Meta Quest 3S virtual reality headset.

data.title: AI mentions in earnings transcripts continue to reach new record highs
data.description:
Morgan Stanley spotlighted that mentions of artificial intelligence (AI) in earnings transcripts continue to reach new record highs, with a primary focus on improving productivity and efficiency.

Numerous examples of AI-driven productivity gains are being shared, showcasing measurable increases in performance. However, an ongoing challenge remains in translating AI usage into substantial revenue growth.

“Our Analysts remain bullish on power demand from AI and are encouraged by higher AI-related capex,” Morgan Stanley said in an investor note:

See what some firms are saying about AI below:

Amazon (AMZN): “AI represents, for sure, the biggest opportunity since cloud and probably the biggest technology shift and opportunity in business since the Internet.”

Expedia (EXPE): “I would say in AI, I would really think about it sort of in three buckets. The first bucket is, how are we using AI to make our products better, whether it's for travelers or for partners? ... The second is looking at changing traveler behaviors. As I remarked, travelers are going to start to search in different ways... And then there's a question of if there are these native AI travel start-ups, could we go partner with them and can we go power them?”

Pinterest (PINS): “The majority of our engineering team uses coding assistance, and 15% of our current code base is generated through AI.

Equifax (EFX): “Leveraging the new Equifax Cloud, we are now on offense with EFX.AI. In 2024, 95% of our new models and scores were built using Equifax AI and machine learning, up from 70% in 2023.”

Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL): “Based on the Nielsen meta-analysis of marketing mix models, on average, Google AI-powered video campaigns on YouTube delivered 17% higher return on advertising spend than manual campaigns.”

Spotify Technologies (SPOT): “And when it comes to DeepSeek, we see this as sort of further evidence that these large AI models and intelligence in general is getting commoditized in open source pretty fast, meaning that the dollar cost of unit per intelligence, if you want to think about it like that, it keeps dropping rapidly and the industry forecast is to drop even more.”

MSCI Inc (MSCI): “These features are clearly delivering value as we're seeing the number of AI interactions increase more than 25x year-over-year. These powerful AI capabilities, along with automation and analytics, are also driving increasing adoption of premium and enterprise editions with sales to high value SKUs up over 40% year-over-year.”

Meta Platforms (META): “So the product transformation and the efficiency story kind of go hand-in-hand. So at present, for example, we've embarked on a scalable sort of mapping of private credit data using AI, which would simply have taken us, first of all, a lot more time. We would have hired -- had to hire a lot more humans.”

F5 Inc (FFIV): “There has been significant early adoption of our first video generation tool that we rolled out in October, Image Animation with hundreds of thousands of advertisers already using it monthly.”

State Street Corp (STT): “Through the use of machine learning, a couple of things have happened. In markets like developed market equities, there are no fund accountants. Machine learning is actually calculating a NAV with every single transaction at the end of the day, at the end of the close of market.”

Citigroup (C):” We accelerated our use of AI, arming 30,000 developers with tools to write code, launched two AI platforms to make 143,000 colleagues more efficient.”

Year-to-date price action: AMZN +4.2%, EXPE +8.6%, EFX -2.1%, PINS +34%, GOOG -1.8%, GOOGL -2.1%, SPOT +42.6%, MSCI -4.5%, META +25.8%, FFIV +23.3%, STT +1%, and C +20.2%.

AI ETFs: (AGIX), (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (ROBT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).

data.title: Ancora seeking US Steel board records, ratcheting up proxy fight - Reuters
data.description:
Activist investor Ancora Holdings, which launched a boardroom challenge at US Steel (NYSE:X) last month, is demanding access to company records from board minutes to financial documents, Reuters reported Tuesday, as it ramps up a campaign to replace some of the steel company's board members and chief executive.

Ancora reportedly told US Steel (NYSE:X) it wants to investigate "potential wrongdoing in connection with ... the company's futile [lawsuit]... and the unusual trading plan of the company's CEO," David Burritt, and is looking for information to determine whether the board violated its fiduciary duties by filing the lawsuit and to determine whether Burritt "sought to trade on material non-public information."

The investor currently owns ~500K shares, or less than 1%, in US Steel (X) but has said it plans to increase its position significantly, has nominated nine director candidates to the steel company's 12-person board, including an executive who could replace Burritt, and wants the company to drop the lawsuit where it is asking a federal appeals court to overturn President Biden's decision to scuttle the merger deal with Nippon Steel.

By pursuing the lawsuit, US Steel (X) is hurting shareholders, Ancora said, noting it wants management and the board to concentrate on fixing the business.

data.title: Ultragenyx granted FDA priority review for gene therapy in rare brain disorder
data.description:
• Ultragenyx Pharma (NASDAQ:RARE) announced Tuesday that the U.S. FDA granted priority review for its marketing application for its gene therapy candidate UX111 (ABO-102) targeted at Sanfilippo syndrome type A, a rare disorder mainly affecting the brain.
  • Its Biologics License Application (BLA) seeking accelerated approval for UX111 is backed by data from the company’s ongoing pivotal Transpher A trial and long-term follow-up studies, Ultragenyx (NASDAQ:RARE) said.
  • Also known as MPS IIIA, Sanfilippo syndrome type A is a lysosomal storage disease with no FDA-approved therapies.
  • “Acceptance of the UX111 BLA brings us closer to being able to provide a first-ever treatment for Sanfilippo syndrome type A,” CEO Emil Kakkis said.
  • In connection with the review, the regulator has said it doesn’t currently plan to hold an advisory committee meeting and issued August 18, 2025, as the target action for the application.

data.title: Broadcom, Taiwan Semi going after Intel has pros and cons: analysts
data.description:
As reports swirl that Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) and Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) could each look to acquire parts of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), there are pros and cons if the deals were to come to fruition, Wall Street analysts said.

Intel shares rose 10% in morning trading on Tuesday, extending recent gains. Taiwan Semiconductor slipped around 1% while Broadcom fell 2.4%.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said such a scenario where Broadcom acquires Intel's chip design and marketing business and Taiwan Semi takes over the manufacturing business would be “time-consuming” and “complicated.” There would be “extensive” approval requirements from global regulators, given Intel's dominant market share in the PC and server CPU spaces, Arya explained.

In addition, there is a “mismatch” between Taiwan Semi and Intel's manufacturing processes, while Taiwan Semiconductor's ongoing expansion plans in Arizona could be impacted to serve customers looking for artificial intelligence processors, Arya added.

Other issues include Broadcom's high-level of debt ($58B in net debt) on its balance sheet and constraints provided by the CHIPs Act on Intel, as it is required to own 50% or more of its manufacturing capabilities as per terms of the agreement.

Pros and cons

Despite the issues related to any potential bid, there are pros and cons for any involvement from Broadcom and Taiwan Semiconductor.

If Broadcom were to get involved, the Hock Tan-led company could use its experience and expertise in increasing the efficiency of assets in “established markets” that have stick customers, Arya said.

“INTC has a very strong brand name and incumbency advantage among enterprise PC and server customers, and could add CPU capability to AVGO who has thus far focused mostly in networking, storage and accelerator silicon,” Arya wrote. “INTC’s CPU could be a possible solution for lower-cost edge AI inference workloads, complementing AVGO’s strength in cloud data centers.”

On the flip side, there is Broadcom's debt levels, the risk of “over-investing” in the x86 ecosystem, extended regulatory approval requirements and the “typical” M&A overhang that could pressure Broadcom's stock.

For Taiwan Semiconductor, there could be issues related to low levels of synergy, cultural differences, varying tool sets, geopolitics, no prior history and potential antitrust concerns, Arya said.

“Separately, if INTC is right about the impending success of its competing 18A manufacturing program, it could be reluctant to give up on it midstream to a key competitor,” Arya added.

Potential outcome?

The situation is still fluid and there are a wide array of potential structures, J.P. Morgan credit analyst Christian Crosby said. However, he believes the “ideal” outcome is for Intel to separate its foundry unit into a new company, which would include Taiwan Semiconductor taking a minority voting stake and operating responsibilities of the combined unit.

“Capital contributions from it and U.S. fabless chip names could ensure the viability of the structure, making a split much more palatable than one without partners, and Intel RemainCo would be in our view better positioned to consider deals with peers as well,” Crosby wrote in a note to clients.

Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse said the most likely scenario is similar to what Taiwan Semiconductor already has in Europe, via its joint venture with Bosch and NXP Semiconductor (NXPI) in the European Semiconductor Manufacturing Company.

"[European Semiconductor Manufacturing Company] is 70% owned by TSMC, and we would expect TSMC to make sure they control the equivalent [joint venture] in the US with at least 51% ownership and 100% control of the asset," Muse wrote in a note to clients. "To help fund the [joint venture], we would expect the U.S. government to participate [perhaps with the U.S. sovereign wealth fund] as well as potential equity commitments from key fabless players in the US including Apple, [Nvidia, AMD, and Qualcomm]."

(This story has been updated with additional analyst commentary.)

data.title: Axsome Therapeutics targets multiple NDA submissions and pipeline advancements through 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Axsome Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AXSM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Herriot Tabuteau highlighted that Axsome has expanded its CNS portfolio to include three commercial products and three late-stage pipeline candidates. The company aims to potentially commercialize seven products by 2027, driven by strong execution across clinical and commercial operations.
  • Total net product revenue reached $386 million for 2024, with CEO Tabuteau stating, "Based on the recent performance, Auvelity is on track to reach blockbuster potential." Symbravo received FDA approval on January 30, 2025, for acute migraine treatment, and preparations for its launch are underway.
  • CFO Nick Pizzie reported total revenues of $118.8 million for Q4, reflecting a 66% year-over-year growth. He noted that "current cash balance is sufficient to fund anticipated operations into cash flow positivity."
  • Chief Commercial Officer Ari Maizel shared that Auvelity achieved 158,000 prescriptions in Q4, marking a 10% sequential growth. Market access for Auvelity improved, covering 78% of all lives and 63% in commercial channels.

Outlook

  • Axsome plans three NDA submissions in 2025: AXS-05 for Alzheimer’s disease agitation, AXS-12 for narcolepsy with cataplexy, and AXS-14 for fibromyalgia. The NDA for AXS-14 is expected this quarter, while filings for AXS-05 and AXS-12 are projected for the second half of 2025.
  • Multiple Phase 3 trial readouts are anticipated in Q1 2025, including EMERGE for Symbravo, FOCUS for Solriamfetol in ADHD, and PARADIGM for Solriamfetol in MDD.
  • Management expects continued growth in 2025, supported by expanded salesforce efforts for Auvelity and the launch of Symbravo.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net product sales totaled $117.3 million, with $92.6 million from Auvelity and $26.2 million from Sunosi. Auvelity’s year-over-year growth was 89%, while Sunosi grew by 16%.
  • Research and development expenses were $55 million in Q4, driven by ongoing Phase 3 trials for Solriamfetol and the advancement of AXS-05 and AXS-12.
  • Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $113.3 million in Q4, reflecting commercialization activities for Auvelity and Sunosi.
  • Net loss for Q4 was $74.9 million, including non-cash charges of $35.9 million.

Q&A

  • Ram Selvaraju, H.C. Wainwright: Questioned the timing and strategy for direct-to-consumer (DTC) promotions for Auvelity and Symbravo. Ari Maizel responded that media spend would align with high patient flow periods in healthcare offices and further details on Symbravo’s commercial plans would follow.
  • Charles Duncan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about synergies between the salesforce expansion for Auvelity and the upcoming Symbravo launch. Maizel explained that the 10%-15% increase in salesforce size would provide overlap in migraine treaters, particularly in primary care settings.
  • David Amsellem, Piper Sandler: Inquired about M&A targets. CFO Nick Pizzie confirmed that Axsome is exploring opportunities that complement its pipeline and leverage its commercial infrastructure.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about Axsome’s portfolio development and commercial efforts, with several questions showing interest in the upcoming NDA filings and product launches. The tone was slightly positive, with specific concerns about market access and scalability strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing the company’s operational efficiency and financial discipline. Statements like "We are very optimistic about the impact of AXS-12" reflected their confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed consistent assurance, while analysts appeared slightly more focused on commercialization challenges for new products.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 revealed expanded revenues, driven by a 10% sequential growth in Auvelity prescriptions compared to a 17% increase in Q3.
  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize the launch of Symbravo and upcoming NDA filings, while Q3 focused more on pipeline trial completions.
  • Analysts in Q4 showed heightened interest in the salesforce expansion and market access improvements for Auvelity, a shift from pipeline-specific queries in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential seasonality effects impacting Q1 2025 gross-to-net discounts for Auvelity and Sunosi, expected to rise to mid-50%.
  • Analyst concerns included competition in the migraine market for Symbravo and payer dynamics for Auvelity. Maizel noted early discussions with payers for Symbravo as "very compelling."
  • Regulatory risks were highlighted, particularly around the timing of NDA submissions for AXS-05 and AXS-12.

Final Takeaway

Axsome Therapeutics showcased strong revenue growth and a robust pipeline during its Q4 2024 earnings call. Key upcoming catalysts include three NDA submissions and multiple late-stage readouts in 2025. Auvelity's growth trajectory, paired with an enhanced salesforce and improved market access, positions the company for continued success. Meanwhile, the launch of Symbravo and advancements in pipeline assets like AXS-05 and AXS-12 are expected to drive further shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BofA's latest survey shows trade war is now biggest concern for fund managers
data.description:
Bank of America's global research team highlighted a significant shift in market sentiment in its latest fund manager survey, noting that the primary tail risk now worrying investors is not an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble, but rather the escalating trade wars initiated by President Donald Trump's tariff moves.

As per the BofA survey, 42% of respondents now see global trade war as the most bearish development for risk assets in 2025, up from January's 30%. Fund managers see "the disorderly" rise in bond yields and federal rate hikes as the second and third-most bearish developments.

BofA's survey also asked fund managers: “Which asset class would fare best under a full-blown trade war scenario?” The overwhelming answer was gold (XAUUSD:CUR) at 58%. The U.S. dollar (DXY) came in second at 15% and bitcoin (BTC-USD) only received 3% of the vote. See the chart below showing the complete breakdown:

Here are some popular exchange-traded funds that track gold, the dollar and bitcoin:

Gold ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GLDM), and (AAAU).

Dollar ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN).

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).
